channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Persona 4 Revival English voice cast announced
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

The new English voices of the protagonist, Yosuke, Chie, Yukiko, and Marie.

ATLUS has announced the English voice cast for Persona 4 Revival. Unlike the Japanese voice cast, which remains the same from previous iterations of Persona 4, the English voice cast is all-new for the remake.

The voice cast is as follows:

  • Protagonist (voiced by Nazeeh Tarsha in English, Daisuke Namikawa in Japanese)
    • A second-year high school student, who, due to unfortunate circumstances with his parents, was sent to live in Inaba at the home of his uncle.
  • Yosuke Hanamura (voiced by Paul Castro Jr. in English, Shoutarou Morikubo in Japanese)
    • Another local high schooler, Yosuke is the cheerful and caring strategist of the group, bringing together the deductions and opinions of team members.
  • Chie Satonaka (voiced by Anne Yatco in English, Yui Horie in Japanese)
    • A lively local girl who attends the town’s high school, Chie is a warm and empathetic person with a strong sense of justice.
  • Yukiko Amagi (voiced by Brianna Knickerbocker in English, Ami Koshimizu in Japanese)
    • A popular student at school, Yukiko is the sole inheritor of one of the town’s long-established inns. She’s reliable and level-headed, albeit spacey.
  • Marie (voiced by Ari Thrash in English, Kana Hanazawa in Japanese)
    • A mysterious girl to encounter in Inaba who occasionally stops through town, Marie comes across as aloof and unfriendly. However, she is also curious and proactive by nature.

Persona 4 Revival is due out for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Microsoft Store on February 18, 2027. It will also be available via Game Pass.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/persona-4-revival-english-voice-cast-announced
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Outward 2 Early Access delayed to 2027
item.content:encoded:
• PC

No longer due out on July 7.

Nine Dots Publishing and developer Nine Dots Studio have delayed the Early Access launch of open-world action RPG Outward 2 from its previously planned July 7 release to 2027. It will be available for PC via Steam, Epic Games Store, and GOG.

Here is the full message from Nine Dots Studio CEO and creative director Guillaume Boucher-Vidal on the delay:

Hi everyone,

I know that this might seem sudden as not that long ago we released a trailer inviting people to explore Aurai during summer, but the truth is that if we just stick to the plan stubbornly, we’re delivering a product that might not satisfy your expectations. The last thing we want to do is disappoint fans of Outward and there has been a lot of comments stating that they wish for the game to spend more time in development before the launch. The point of a Beta is to listen to feedback, and so we will.

Dropping this news is especially painful knowing that some of our most dedicated players actually planned their summer break around the release date. It sucks for you and I personally apologize to everyone affected this way. However, it would be even worse if you ended up playing something over your vacation that you were not even happy with.

It’s hard to gauge what is the appropriate degree of stability expected for Early Access. The best we can do is take a hard, honest look at the game, and at our team, and ask: in a few weeks, will this be good enough to charge money for? Our aim is to have such a stark jump between this Beta and the Early Access launch that we earn the trust of our players. You were there for the first Outward and that’s why we were successful. We want you to be happy to be there again for Outward 2.

Thank you to everyone who participated in the Beta and our earlier playtests, who provided feedback, who shared our news, talked with us directly during streams or on Discord. I apologize that we couldn’t give you the game you guys deserve on time. I guess that’s the game development’s equivalent of a defeat scenario: a setback and we come back better prepared.

Guillaume a.k.a. Gheeyom
CEO and Creative Director



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/outward-2-early-access-delayed-to-2027
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Leafy Corner launches July 30
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Xbox One
  • Xbox Series

Cozy shop management game dated.

Cozy shop management game Leafy Corner will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Xbox One, Switch, and PC via Steam and Microsoft Store on July 30, developer Fireline Games announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Fireline Games:

About

Run the plant shop of your dreams in Leafy Corner, a cozy shop management game where you grow, sell, and care for real-life houseplants!

Step into the role of a plant shop owner. Grow plants, serve your customers, and create a warm, inviting space filled with greenery and charm.

Watch your plants grow over time, unfolding leaf by leaf as you care for them.

Not everyone knows what they’re looking for. Sometimes customers bring unusual or tricky requests. Finding just the right plant to fit their needs adds a fun challenge.

Decorate your shop with a variety of unlockable items, from shelves to plant pots. Pick colors that fit your mood and create a space that reflects your unique style.

Key Features

  • Grow, care for, and sell over 100 unique plants.
  • Discover plant variegations.
  • Build and decorate your shop with a variety of pots, furniture, and decoration options.
  • Fulfill orders and help customers.
  • Complete tasks to progress your shop.
  • Increase your shop’s reputation to unlock new content.
  • Customize your character.

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/leafy-corner-launches-july-30
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
